title: Cutting Parameters Tool Life Calculator
description: Calculates tool life based on cutting speed, feed rate, depth of cut, and material properties, with adjustments for process reliability and hidden loss drivers.

inputs.label: Cutting Speed
inputs.businessContext: Surface speed of the workpiece relative to the cutting tool, based on ISO 3685.

businessContext_i18n.en: Surface speed of the workpiece relative to the cutting tool, based on ISO 3685.

inputs.label: Feed Rate
inputs.businessContext: Linear distance the tool advances per revolution, affects chip load and tool wear.

businessContext_i18n.en: Linear distance the tool advances per revolution, affects chip load and tool wear.

inputs.label: Depth of Cut
inputs.businessContext: Radial or axial engagement of the tool, influences cutting forces and heat generation.

businessContext_i18n.en: Radial or axial engagement of the tool, influences cutting forces and heat generation.

inputs.label: Tool Material
inputs.businessContext: Material of the cutting tool, affects wear resistance and optimal cutting parameters.

businessContext_i18n.en: Material of the cutting tool, affects wear resistance and optimal cutting parameters.

inputs.label: Workpiece Material
inputs.businessContext: Material being machined, determines machinability rating and recommended speeds.

businessContext_i18n.en: Material being machined, determines machinability rating and recommended speeds.

inputs.label: Coolant Used
inputs.businessContext: Indicates if coolant is applied, reduces thermal stress and extends tool life.

businessContext_i18n.en: Indicates if coolant is applied, reduces thermal stress and extends tool life.

inputs.label: Machine Stability Factor
inputs.businessContext: Factor representing machine rigidity and vibration damping, based on condition monitoring data.

businessContext_i18n.en: Factor representing machine rigidity and vibration damping, based on condition monitoring data.

rules.message: Cutting speed to feed rate ratio is too low, risk of built-up edge formation.
Depth of cut exceeds recommended limit for superalloys, tool failure likely.
Ceramic tools should not be used with coolant due to thermal shock risk.

thresholds.message: Tool life is critically low (<10 minutes), consider reducing cutting speed or feed.
Immediate action required: tool life below 5 minutes, stop machining and review parameters.

formulas.name: Base Tool Life (Taylor's Equation)
formulas.description: Calculates theoretical tool life using Taylor's extended equation with material-specific constants.

formulas.name: Material Adjustment Factor
formulas.description: Adjusts base tool life based on workpiece material machinability rating.

parameters.machinability_rating: steel:1.0, stainless_steel:0.6, aluminum:3.0, titanium:0.4, cast_iron:1.2, superalloy:0.3

formulas.name: Tool Material Adjustment Factor
formulas.description: Adjusts tool life based on tool material wear resistance.

formulas.name: Coolant Adjustment Factor
formulas.description: Adjusts tool life based on coolant usage.

formulas.name: Machine Stability Adjustment
formulas.description: Adjusts tool life based on machine condition and stability.

formulas.name: Adjusted Tool Life
formulas.description: Combines all adjustment factors to compute final tool life in minutes.
formulas.formula: T_adjusted = T_base * K_material * K_tool * K_coolant * K_machine

formulas.name: Primary Result (Tool Life)
formulas.description: Final tool life in minutes, rounded to two decimal places.

primary.label: Tool Life
primary.description: Estimated tool life under given cutting parameters.

base_tool_life.label: Base Tool Life (Taylor)
base_tool_life.description: Theoretical tool life from Taylor's equation.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Vibration-Induced Wear
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Excessive vibration accelerates flank wear and chipping, reducing tool life by up to 30%.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Thermal Cracking Risk
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Intermittent cutting or improper coolant application can cause thermal cracks, especially in ceramics.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Built-Up Edge Formation
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Low cutting speeds and sticky materials cause material adhesion, degrading surface finish and tool life.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Tool Micro-Chipping
hiddenLossDrivers.description: High feed rates and unstable conditions lead to micro-chipping of the cutting edge.

suggestedActions.label: Reduce Cutting Speed
suggestedActions.description: Decrease cutting speed by 10-15% to extend tool life, especially if current speed is near upper limit.

suggestedActions.label: Optimize Feed Rate
suggestedActions.description: Adjust feed rate to balance chip load and tool wear; consider reducing if >0.3 mm/rev.

suggestedActions.label: Apply Coolant
suggestedActions.description: Ensure coolant is used to reduce thermal stress and flush chips away from cutting zone.

suggestedActions.label: Check Machine Stability
suggestedActions.description: Inspect machine for wear, backlash, or vibration issues; consider maintenance if stability factor <0.8.

suggestedActions.label: Use Stronger Tool Material
suggestedActions.description: Switch to a more wear-resistant tool material (e.g., carbide to ceramic) if tool life is below target.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Score
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Confidence level in the calculated tool life based on input accuracy and process variability.
dataConfidenceAdjusted.calculation: base_confidence = 85; if coolant_used == false then -5; if machine_stability_factor < 0.8 then -10; if workpiece_material == 'superalloy' then -5; final = max(50, base_confidence - deductions)
